Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel- Civil
Off Calendar Aug-22-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar May-13-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Murphy for (petitioner) T. Anne Lassahn. Yulissa Zalaica for (beneficiary/petitioner) Diana Lopez.

The Court noted that there is no appearance by Kevin Lopez. Mr. Murphy stated that he filed a dismissal this morning. The Court asked if everyone was on board with the appointment of Ms. Lassahn. Ms. Zulaica stated that she will withdraw the petition of Ms. Lopez. The Court granted the petition for Letters of Administration, signed the order in open court and stated that a bond in the amount of $1,150,000.00 would be required. The petitions for letters of administration filed by Kevin Lopez and Diana Lopez are off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputies Lopes & Santiago. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-24-2015 Continued To Apr-07-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Deb Kinney for (respondent & beficiary) Diana Allred Lopez. James Murphy for (proposed administrator) Anne Lassahn.

Mr. Murphy reported that Ms. Lassahn has agreed to take the case, they will be preparing the petition and expect to have it filed within a week and that they do not anticipate any objections to the petition. Ms. Kinney stated that they will withdraw petition/objections when new petition is filed. The 2 Petitions for Letters of Administration were continued to April 7, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that they will be withdrawn when the petition is filed by the neutral administrator.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-24-2015 Continued To Apr-07-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-03-2015 Continued To Mar-24-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Randall Witte for (proposed administrator) Kevin Lopez. Daniel Redman for and with (proposed administrator) Dianna Lopez.

Mr. Witte reported that they are defaulting to a neutral administrator, that he will continue to represent Mr. Lopez in terms of the petition for administration but does not know if he will represent Mr. Lopez as a beneficiary. Mr. Redman stated that the proposed neutral administrators are Debra Dolch and Anne Lasanne and that Ms. Lopez will be meeting with them and deciding which candidate is appropriate. The 2 petitions for letters of administration were continued to March 24, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that a new petition will be filed and these petitions will be taken off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-27-2015 Continued To Mar-03-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Park. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-27-2015 Continued To Mar-03-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Randall Witte for and with Kevin Lopez (Petitioner). Daniel Redman and Yulissa Zulaica for and with Dianna Lopez (Petitioner/Objector).

The Court stated that this is a situation that cries out for an agreement to have a neutral appointed and raised the issue of conflict either Party would have to deal with if appointed. Counsel stated that they believe an agreement can be reached, that they are making progress, they do not want to have a neutral fiduciary appointed and that they need 30 to 60-days. Both Petitions for Letters of Administration were continued to March 3, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court encouraged the Parties to have something worked out by then or have a plan on how to proceed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Park. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-21-2014 Continued To Dec-02-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Randall Witte for Kevin Lopez. Daniel Redman for and with Dianna Lopez.

The Court noted that it received the objection yesterday at 4:30 p.m. Mr. Redman stated that over the last month there has been some fact gathering and discussion on various issues, and that his Client and two disabled siblings live in the home. Mr. Witte stated that his Client is not opposed to the family remaining in the home, that it is a matter of working out the issues and that the Granddaughter wants to be paid out. The Court advised the Parties that Pro Bono Mediation is available and raised the issue of having a private fiduciary step in. The Petition for Letters of Administration was continued to December 2, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Lopez' Petition for Letters of Administration on calendar for November 19, 2014 will also be continued to December 2, 2014 when reviewed by the Probate Examiner.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
